components.name: disodium hydrogen phosphate (Na2HPO4)
components.source: cited:standard M9 (CSH Protocols M9 5X salts; Sambrook & Russell)
components.evidence: Main text: 'Cells were grown on M9 minimal media, supplemented with 34 mg/l chloramphenicol and suitable carbon sources.' The paper never redefines M9, so the base salts are the canonical M9 composition (5X M9 salts: Na2HPO4·7H2O 64 g/L → final 12.8 g/L).

components.name: monopotassium phosphate (KH2PO4)
components.source: cited:standard M9 (CSH Protocols M9 5X salts; Sambrook & Russell)
components.evidence: Standard M9 5X salts: KH2PO4 15 g/L → final 3 g/L (22 mM). Paper uses undefined 'M9 minimal media'.

components.name: sodium chloride (NaCl)
components.source: cited:standard M9 (CSH Protocols M9 5X salts; Sambrook & Russell)
components.evidence: Standard M9 5X salts: NaCl 2.5 g/L → final 0.5 g/L (8.56 mM).

components.name: ammonium chloride (NH4Cl)
components.source: cited:standard M9 (CSH Protocols M9 5X salts; Sambrook & Russell)
components.evidence: Standard M9 5X salts: NH4Cl 5 g/L → final 1 g/L (18.7 mM). Sole N source in M9.

components.name: magnesium sulfate (MgSO4)
components.evidence: CSH M9 recipe: 'MgSO4 (1 M) 200 µL' per 100 mL → 2 mM final.

components.name: calcium chloride (CaCl2)
components.evidence: CSH M9 recipe: 'CaCl2 (1 M) 10 µL' per 100 mL → 0.1 mM final.

components.name: sodium pyruvate
components.evidence: Main text: 'Cells were propagated in M9 minimal media supplemented with non-labeled pyruvate (5 g/L)'. Suppl. Exp. Procedures: 'Samples were grown in 500 mL glass flasks in M9 minimal media supplemented with sodium pyruvate [5 g/L].'

components.name: chloramphenicol
components.evidence: 'Cells were grown on M9 minimal media, supplemented with 34 mg/l chloramphenicol and suitable carbon sources, as specified in the text.'

components.name: carbon dioxide (CO2, elevated atmosphere)
components.concentration: pCO2 = 0.1 atm (10% CO2 in gas phase)
components.evidence: 'All growth was performed in enriched CO2 atmosphere (pCO2 = 0.1 atm).' Labeling gas mix: '10% 13CO2, 10% O2, and 80% N2.' Elevated CO2 is required for growth of the CBB-cycle strain.

components.name: oxygen (O2)
components.evidence: Gas mixture used: '10% 13CO2, 10% O2, and 80% N2'; growth in a gas-controlled shaking incubator at 37°C. Growth is aerobic.

media.base_medium: Standard M9 minimal medium (Cold Spring Harbor Protocols / Sambrook & Russell) supplemented with sodium pyruvate (5 g/L) as sole organic carbon/energy source, 34 mg/L chloramphenicol, under an elevated CO2 atmosphere (pCO2 = 0.1 atm).

sources_chased.what: Supplemental Experimental Procedures (mmc1.pdf) to confirm the medium and pyruvate concentration
sources_chased.how: EuropePMC REST supplementaryFiles zip (https://www.ebi.ac.uk/europepmc/webservices/rest/PMC4930481/supplementaryFiles) -> extracted mmc1.pdf -> pdftotext
added: confirmed M9 base is left undefined (no trace elements / vitamins stated)

sources_chased.what: Canonical M9 minimal medium base-salt composition (paper cites no base-medium reference and never lists salt concentrations)
sources_chased.how: WebFetch CSH Protocols M9 recipe + WebSearch standard M9 5X salts

missing_before: disodium hydrogen phosphate (Na2HPO4) as explicit reagent + concentration
ammonium chloride (NH4Cl) as N source + concentration
elevated CO2 atmosphere (pCO2 = 0.1 atm) as inorganic carbon source
media.notes: The paper (Antonovsky et al., Cell 2016, 'Sugar Synthesis from CO2 in E. coli') and its Supplemental Experimental Procedures use undefined 'M9 minimal media' with no base-medium citation; base-salt concentrations are therefore the canonical M9 recipe. IMPORTANT: neither the paper nor standard M9 includes any trace-element mix (Co, Cu, Mn, Zn) or vitamins/thiamine, so the Co2+/Cu2+/Mn2+/Zn2+/Fe2+/Fe3+ entries in current_components are genome-scale-model open-exchange artifacts (lb -1000), NOT documented recipe compounds, and were intentionally not carried into the recipe. Iron is not specified in standard M9 and is not listed. This is the batch (flask/plate) pyruvate condition; the chemostat feed additionally contained 25-100 mg/L xylose (a separate medium).
